summary: Claude via the REAL mcpbridge вҶ’ executor вҶ’ real execute_shell_command (cat a pre-seeded secret), streamed at the mcpagent layer
review_criteria: proper formatting вҖ” clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable вҖ” reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

issues: mcpagent StreamingChunkEvent MIXES raw terminal-snapshot frames with clean transcript content, with no field to tell them apart. Of 13 content events, only 2 are genuine assistant transcript text ('I'll read the secret file from the specified path.' + the secret); the rest are pane frames. My ANSI (\x1b) heuristic even let a plain-text pane frame through as 'clean' (the 3rd clean chunk is a de-ANSI'd Claude banner). A no-terminal UI (message modes 2/3) consuming StreamingChunkEvent therefore gets terminal noise interleaved with the real message. Fix at the mcpagent layer: carry the provider's stream_source on the event (or route terminal snapshots to a distinct event type) so consumers can select transcript-only. This is the concrete message-modes-layer gap.
agent_review.notes: PRODUCTION-FIDELITY PROOF (the point of this test): the turn went through the REAL path вҖ” Claude Code вҶ’ real mcpbridge binary вҶ’ executor HTTP API вҶ’ real execute_shell_command running an actual shell вҶ’ `cat secret.txt`. Evidence: (1) tool_names=[mcp__api-bridge__execute_shell_command], the real bridge tool, streamed as a ToolCallStartEvent; (2) the answer is REALBRIDGE_SECRET_ffbbc33daaac, a token that existed ONLY in a pre-seeded file and NEVER in the prompt вҖ” impossible to answer without actually executing the tool through the bridge; (3) genuine clean transcript content streamed alongside ('I'll read the secret file from the specified path.'). Signed good: the real-bridge streaming path is proven end to end. The terminal/transcript mixing above is the tracked follow-up, not a failure of this path.
